page: Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a Bare Metal SSD server powered by an AMD EPYC 9354P processor built on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration features 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs with a base clock speed of 3.25 GHz, supported by a shared CPU allocation. It includes 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, which provides 12.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 960 GB local SSD, and the system does not include any GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong processing and memory capabilities. The server achieves top-tier status in single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 3179.99, while multi-core performance reaches 101731.42. Memory performance is highly competitive, with a PassMark memory score in the top 10% and uncached read bandwidth reaching 351.41 GB/sec. Database operations are a notable strength, scoring 32639.26 in PassMark database benchmarks, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. Additionally, the server processes Redis SET operations at 91.34 million operations per second.

The high memory density of 12.0 GB per core and DDR5 architecture offer qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads, minimizing the need to scale out to multiple nodes. However, the shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring strict, isolated physical core execution. For LLM inference, the server demonstrates strong capabilities, processing 135M model prompts at 4846.38 tokens per second and 7B model text generation at 55.29 tokens per second. This profile makes the server suitable for large-scale database hosting, memory-intensive caching layers, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a Bare Metal SSD server featuring an AMD EPYC 9354P processor with 32 physical cores, 64 vCPUs, and a shared CPU allocation running at 3.25 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz and a 960 GB local SSD, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in single-core CPU execution and database operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth and multi-core capabilities. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 12.0 GB per core provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive deployments. This server is suited for database hosting, Redis caching, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring 64 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 960 GB SSD. It delivers top-tier database and memory performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a bare metal server with 64 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, delivering top-tier database performance.
tagline: A high-density bare metal server featuring AMD EPYC processing, massive DDR5 memory, and top-tier database performance for enterprise workloads.

768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
